title: Yellowstone via Gallatin Gateway Montana
author: Chicago, Milwaukee, St. Paul, and Pacific Railroad Company

quoteFirst: For example, it is high water to-day at noon, it will be low water at
quoteTime: eleven minutes after six in the evening
quoteLast: ; and, consequently, after two changes more, the time of high water the next day will be at about three quarters of an hour after noon; the day following it will be at about half an hour after one, the day following that at a quarter past two, and so on for thirty days; when it will again be found to be high water at noon, as on the day the observation was first made.
title: The Library of Work and Play: Mechanics, Indoors and Out
